title: Eat Less on Rest Days
hook: You need to eat less on rest days
science_text: Rest days are when your body repairs. Dropping food significantly slows recovery.

title: Sports Drinks Beat Water in Practice
hook: Sports drinks are better than water during practice
science_text: For sessions under 60-75 minutes, water is enough. Sports drinks matter for long matches and tournaments, not every training session.

title: Eating Fat Makes You Fat
science_text: Healthy fats (eggs, nuts, avocado) support joint health and hormone development — critical for 12-18 year olds still growing.

title: Skip Breakfast to Save Calories
science_text: A player who skips breakfast trains on empty. Muscle gets broken down for fuel instead.

title: Red Meat Is Bad for Athletes
hook: Red meat is bad for athletes
science_text: Lean red meat is one of the best sources of iron and zinc — both directly tied to energy and immune function.

title: Hydrate Only on Match Day
hook: You only need to hydrate on match day
science_text: Hydration is cumulative. Showing up dehydrated to a match because you didn't drink enough the day before is very common in youth players.

title: More Protein Always Means More Muscle
hook: More protein always means more muscle
science_text: Excess protein beyond what the body can use gets stored as fat or excreted. Total calories and training load determine muscle gain.

title: Fruit Has Too Much Sugar
science_text: The sugar in whole fruit comes with fiber, water, and micronutrients. It behaves completely differently in the body than added sugar.

title: Skip Eating After a Late-Night Game
hook: You don't need to eat after a late-night game
science_text: The recovery window after a match is when muscles reload glycogen. Skipping it means arriving to the next session already behind.

title: Tired Means More Sleep, Not More Food
hook: Feeling tired means you need more sleep, not food
science_text: Fatigue in youth athletes is often under-fueling. Low energy intake is one of the most under-diagnosed issues in the 12-18 age group.
